title: Koeleria pungens
intro: Koeleria pungens, commonly known as bluebunch wheatgrass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria pungens include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Koeleria pungens is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Koeleria pungens is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is commonly known as prairie Junegrass.
